briefdescription.para: events are propagated to parents in the traditional way
events are propagated based on geometry.
Touch event propagation way.
Event Processing finished signal type.
Key event signal type.
key event generated signal type
Touch signal type.
WheelEvent signal type.
WheelEvent generated signal type.
Create an initialized Scene handle.

parameterdescription.para: The size of the set surface for this scene
The rotated angle of the set surface for this scene
The rotated angle of the screen

simplesect.para: a handle to a newly allocated Dali resource.

briefdescription.para: Downcast an Object handle to Scene handle.

detaileddescription.para:
If handle points to a Scene object the downcast produces valid handle. If not the returned handle is left uninitialized.
handle
to An object
handle to a Scene object or an uninitialized handle

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the Scene that the given actor belongs to.

simplesect.para: The Scene.

briefdescription.para: Create an uninitialized Scene handle.

detaileddescription.para: This can be initialized with Scene::New(). Calling member functions with an uninitialized Dali::Object is not allowed.

briefdescription.para: Destructor.

detaileddescription.para: This is non-virtual since derived Handle types must not contain data or virtual methods.

briefdescription.para: This copy constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the copied handle

briefdescription.para: This assignment operator is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the copied handle

simplesect.para: A reference to this

briefdescription.para: This move constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the moved handle

briefdescription.para: This move assignment operator is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the moved handle

simplesect.para: A reference to this

briefdescription.para: Adds a child Actor to the Scene.

detaileddescription.para:
The child will be referenced.
actor
The child
The actor has been initialized.
The actor does not have a parent.

briefdescription.para: Removes a child Actor from the Scene.

detaileddescription.para:
The child will be unreferenced.
actor
The child
The actor has been added to the stage.

briefdescription.para: Shows the scene if it is hidden. @SINCE_2_3.35.
Hides the scene if it is showing. @SINCE_2_3.35.
Returns whether the window is visible or not. @SINCE_2_3.35.

simplesect.para: True if the window is visible, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Informs the scene need full update. @SINCE_2_4.42.
Requests forced rendering for this scene.

parameterdescription.para: The number of frames to render forcibly. @SINCE_2_5.17

briefdescription.para: Returns the size of the Scene in pixels as a Vector.

detaileddescription.para: The x component will be the width of the Scene in pixels. The y component will be the height of the Scene in pixels.

simplesect.para: The size of the Scene as a Vector

detaileddescription.para:
Sets horizontal and vertical pixels per inch value that is used by the display
dpi
Horizontal and vertical dpi value

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the DPI of the display device to which the scene is connected.

simplesect.para: The horizontal and vertical DPI

briefdescription.para: Sets the background color.

parameterdescription.para: The new background color

briefdescription.para: Gets the background color of the render surface.

simplesect.para: The background color

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the list of render-tasks.

simplesect.para: A valid handle to a RenderTaskList

briefdescription.para: Returns the Scene's Root Layer.

simplesect.para: The root layer

briefdescription.para: Returns the Scene's Overlay Layer. If there is no overlay layer yet, this creates the layer and an associated render task.

simplesect.para: The overlay layer

briefdescription.para: Queries the number of on-stage layers.

detaileddescription.para:
Note that a default layer is always provided (count >= 1).
The number of layers

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the layer at a specified depth.

parameterdescription.para: The depth

simplesect.para: The layer found at the given depth
Depth is less than layer count; see GetLayerCount().

briefdescription.para: Informs the scene that the set surface has been resized.

parameterdescription.para: The new width of the set surface
The new height of the set surface

briefdescription.para: Informs the scene that the surface has been replaced.
Removes the scene graph object.
Discards this Scene from the Core.
Sets the render target for the surface.

parameterdescription.para: The render target create info for the surface

detaileddescription.para:
This function is called when an event is queued.
event
A event to queue.
This function is called by Core when events are processed.

briefdescription.para: Adds a callback that is called when the frame rendering is done by the graphics driver.

parameterdescription.para: The function to call
The Id to specify the frame. It will be passed when the callback is called.

simplesect.para: A callback of the following type may be used: voidMyFunction(int32_tframeId); This callback will be deleted once it is called.
Ownership of the callback is passed onto this class.

briefdescription.para: Adds a callback that is called when the frame is displayed on the display.

parameterdescription.para: The function to call
The Id to specify the frame. It will be passed when the callback is called.

simplesect.para: A callback of the following type may be used: voidMyFunction(int32_tframeId); This callback will be deleted once it is called.
Ownership of the callback is passed onto this class.

briefdescription.para: Gets the callback list that is called when the frame rendering is done by the graphics driver.

parameterdescription.para: The callback list

simplesect.para: This is called in the update thread.

briefdescription.para: Gets the callback list that is called when the frame is displayed on the display.

parameterdescription.para: The callback list

simplesect.para: This is called in the update thread.

memberdef.argsstring: (float width, float height, int32_t windowOrientation, int32_t screenOrientation)
memberdef.name: SurfaceRotated

briefdescription.para: Informs the scene that the set surface has been rotated.

parameterdescription.para: The width of rotated surface
The height of rotated surface
the current window orientation
the current screen orientation

briefdescription.para: Gets the current surface orientation. It gets the value from the scene object.

simplesect.para: The current surface orientation.

briefdescription.para: Gets the current screen orientation. It gets the value from the scene object.

simplesect.para: The current screen orientation.

briefdescription.para: Gets the current surface rectangle. It gets the value from the scene object.

simplesect.para: The current surface rectangle

detaileddescription.para:
Query how many times the surface rect changed.
It will reset surface rect changed count.
The count of the surface rect changed.

briefdescription.para: Send message to acknowledge for completing window rotation with current window orientation.

detaileddescription.para: If this function is called, the message is sent to render thread, then mSurfaceRectChanged in scene-graph-scene is set with true. After that, render thread checks whether window rotation event is received, mSurfaceRectChanged and the neccessary flag are set. If they are all true, rotation done function is called to complete window rotation.

briefdescription.para: Query wheter is set to acknowledge for completing surface rotation.

detaileddescription.para:
true it should be acknowledged.
If SetRotationCompletedAcknowledgement() is called and the related message is received to scene-graph-scene, then mSurfaceRectChanged in scene-graph-scene is set with true.
When this function is called, the mSurfaceRectChanged in scene-graph-scene is return. Then, the flag will be reset.

simplesect.para: This function should be not called the application's main thread. Because this function should be called in windwow surface's postrender() function to complete window rotation manually.

briefdescription.para: Keep rendering for at least the given amount of time.

detaileddescription.para: By default, Dali will stop rendering when no Actor positions are being set, and when no animations are running etc. This method is useful to force screen refreshes.

parameterdescription.para: Time to keep rendering, 0 means render at least one more frame

briefdescription.para: Sets whether the scene will update partial area or full area.

parameterdescription.para: True if the scene should update partial area

simplesect.para: This doesn't change the global value which is set by the environment variable. This works when partial update is enabled by the environment variable. If the partial update is disabled by the environment variable, it changes nothing.

briefdescription.para: Queries whether the scene will update partial area.

simplesect.para: True if the scene should update partial area

briefdescription.para: Sets whether the processes using geometry event propagation touch and hover events.

parameterdescription.para: True if the processes using geometry event propagation touch and hover events.

briefdescription.para: Queries whether the scene using geometry event propagation touch and hover events.

simplesect.para: True if the scene using geometry event propagation touch and hover events.

briefdescription.para: Get the Touch Propagation Type.

simplesect.para: TouchPropagationType

briefdescription.para: Sets the native window id.

parameterdescription.para: The native window id

briefdescription.para: Gets the native window id.

simplesect.para: The native window id

briefdescription.para: Gets the last pan gesture state.
This signal is emitted just after the event processing is finished.

simplesect.para: The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when key event is received.

detaileddescription.para:
A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(constKeyEvent&event);
The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: The user would connect to this signal to get a KeyEvent when KeyEvent is generated.

detaileddescription.para: If the control already consumed key event, KeyEventProcessor do not need to Emit keyEvent. Therefore, KeyinputManager first checks whether KeyEvent is generated as KeyEventGeneratedSignal. After that keyEventProcessor must invoke KeyEvent only if KeyEventGeneratedSignal () is not consumed.
A callback of the following type may be connected: boolYourCallbackName(constKeyEvent&event);

simplesect.para: The return is true if KeyEvent is consumed, otherwise false.

briefdescription.para: The user would connect to this signal to intercept a KeyEvent at window.

detaileddescription.para: Intercepts KeyEvents in the window before dispatching KeyEvents to the control. If a KeyEvent is consumed, no KeyEvent is delivered to the control.
A callback of the following type may be connected: boolYourCallbackName(constKeyEvent&event);

simplesect.para: The return is true if KeyEvent is consumed, otherwise false.

briefdescription.para: The user would connect to this signal to monitor a KeyEvent at window. This signal is emitted when KeyEvent is generated.

detaileddescription.para: A Callback of the following type may be connected: boolYourCallbackName(constKeyEvent&event);

simplesect.para: The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when the screen is touched and when the touch ends (i.e. the down & up touch events only).

detaileddescription.para: If there are multiple touch points, then this will be emitted when the first touch occurs and then when the last finger is lifted. An interrupted event will also be emitted (if it occurs). A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(TouchEventevent);

simplesect.para: The touch signal to connect to
Motion events are not emitted.

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when wheel event is received.

detaileddescription.para:
A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(constWheelEvent&event);
The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: When a custom wheel event occurs, it need to process the focused actor first.

detaileddescription.para: Therefore, KeyboardFocusManager first checks whether WheelEvent is generated as WheelEventGeneratedSignal. After that wheelEventProcessor must invoke WheelEvent only if wheelEventGeneratedSignal () is not consumed.
This is only valid for custom wheel events.
A callback of the following type may be connected: boolYourCallbackName(constWheelEvent&event);

simplesect.para: The return is true if WheelEvent is consumed, otherwise false.

briefdescription.para: This constructor is used by Dali::New() methods.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to an internal Scene resource

briefdescription.para: Scene creates a "world" that can be bound to a surface for rendering.
